description:
You are a FinOps analyst asked to prototype a tiny cost-analysis tool on a fresh Linux machine.
    Complete every step from project layout to final report **inside the terminal**.
    
    Starting point
    1. A small AWS cost-and-usage CSV is already on disk:  
       • /home/user/cloud_data/cost_data.csv  
       (do NOT move or rename it)
    2. You have normal user privileges (no sudo).

    Final goal
    Deliver a parsed, human-readable cost summary while keeping the whole work neatly isolated in a Python virtual environment created with the standard library module “venv”.

    Required end state
    1. Virtual environment  
       • Absolute path: /home/user/finops_venv  
       • Must be created with the built-in venv module (not conda, pipenv, poetry, virtualenv, etc.).  
       • Inside the venv, install exactly these packages (and their transitive deps):  
         ‑ pandas  (any recent version)  
         ‑ boto3   (simulate later cloud API work)  
       • Freeze the full, resolved dependency list into the text file /home/user/cloud_data/requirements.txt by using “pip freeze …”.
    
    2. Python analysis script  
       • Path: /home/user/cloud_data/analyze_costs.py  
       • Purpose: read cost_data.csv, compute total cost and cost per service, and then write the summary log described below.  
       • The script may be as short or long as you prefer but MUST run successfully with `python analyze_costs.py` **while the venv is activated**.
    
    3. Summary log  
       • Path: /home/user/cloud_data/cloud_costs_summary.log  
       • Format that the automated grader will check (each item on its own line, no spaces except those inside the service names, and a trailing newline at the end of the file):
            TOTAL_COST,<total_cost_two_decimals>
            SERVICE_COST,EC2,<cost_two_decimals>
            SERVICE_COST,RDS,<cost_two_decimals>
            SERVICE_COST,S3,<cost_two_decimals>
         ‑ Use exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
         ‑ Lines must appear in the order shown above.  
         ‑ Do not include any extra lines, headers, or blank lines.
    
    4. Directory hygiene  
       • Do not leave __pycache__ directories under /home/user/cloud_data.  
       • Do not commit any credentials, tokens, or environment variables to disk.
    
    Helpful reminders
    • “python -m venv /home/user/finops_venv” is the canonical way to create a venv.  
    • After the venv is active, the interpreter should be “which python → /home/user/finops_venv/bin/python”.  
    • pandas can read a CSV with: `pd.read_csv("cost_data.csv")`.  
    • Aggregation hint: `df.groupby("Service")["CostUSD"].sum()`.

    What to deliver
    When you finish, these artefacts must exist exactly as specified:
    • /home/user/finops_venv/ … (entire virtual-env tree)  
    • /home/user/cloud_data/analyze_costs.py  
    • /home/user/cloud_data/cloud_costs_summary.log  
    • /home/user/cloud_data/requirements.txt

    The automated test will:
    1. Confirm the venv directory exists and “python” inside it runs.  
    2. `source /home/user/finops_venv/bin/activate && pip list` must show pandas and boto3.  
    3. Read requirements.txt and make sure it contains frozen versions of pandas and boto3.  
    4. Execute the script and compare the freshly produced cloud_costs_summary.log to the required output format (byte-for-byte).  
    5. Ensure no __pycache__ directory exists under /home/user/cloud_data.

    Perform all necessary terminal operations to achieve the final state.
truth:
Initial files already present before the agent starts:
    • /home/user/cloud_data/cost_data.csv
      ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
      │Service,Date,CostUSD                           │
      │EC2,2023-07-01,25.50                           │
      │S3,2023-07-01,5.25                             │
      │RDS,2023-07-01,12.00                           │
      │EC2,2023-07-02,30.75                           │
      │S3,2023-07-02,4.75                             │
      │RDS,2023-07-02,11.25                           │
      │EC2,2023-07-03,28.00                           │
      │S3,2023-07-03,6.00                             │
      └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    Correct aggregation values:
      EC2  → 84.25
      S3   → 16.00
      RDS  → 23.25
      TOTAL→ 123.50

    Therefore the grader expects
    • /home/user/cloud_data/cloud_costs_summary.log to contain exactly:  
      TOTAL_COST,123.50\n
      SERVICE_COST,EC2,84.25\n
      SERVICE_COST,RDS,23.25\n
      SERVICE_COST,S3,16.00\n

    (That is four lines, each ending with a UNIX newline \n, and no blank lines afterwards.)

    The test harness will also verify:
    1. Directory /home/user/finops_venv exists and contains bin/activate.
    2. Inside requirements.txt there are lines starting with “pandas==” and “boto3==”.
    3. No directory named “__pycache__” exists anywhere under /home/user/cloud_data.
